task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |

display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V2_Temp_0.1/test14/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    <geom type="plane" size="1 1 .1" rgba=".9 .9 .9 1"/>

    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Sphere, Diameter 0.1 (Radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <!-- Bob position: Rod length (0.2485) + radius (0.05) = 0.2985 from hinge? 
           Wait, the rod is 0.2485. If the rod is from [0,0,0] to [0.2485,0,0], 
           the center of the sphere should be at [0.2485 + 0.05, 0, 0] = [0.2985, 0, 0] 
           if the rod is just a connection. 
           Actually, usually the rod length is the distance from hinge to center of bob.
           Let's assume rod length 0.2485 is the distance from hinge to the center of the bob.
           Or the rod itself is 0.2485.
           Re-reading: "Rod: Length: 0.2485 m". 
           If rod is 0.2485, and bob diameter is 0.1 (radius 0.05).
           Center of bob would be at 0.2485 + 0.05 = 0.2985.
           BUT "Bob positioned along +x from hinge" and "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
           Vertical is +y. +90 degrees from vertical (downwards? or upwards?)
           Usually vertical is [0, -1, 0] (gravity is [0, -9.81, 0]).
           If gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], vertical is [0, -1, 0].
           +90 degrees from vertical (downwards) would be horizontal.
           If vertical is [0, 1, 0], +90 degrees is [1, 0, 0].
           The prompt says: +y = upward.
           So vertical is +y. 
           "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical" -> if vertical is [0,1,0], then [1,0,0].
           Let's place the bob at [0.2985, 1, 0] if the rod is 0.2485 and bob radius is 0.05.
           Wait, if rod length is 0.2485, maybe it's the distance to the bob center.
           Let's use rod as a geom from [0,0,0] to [0.2485,0,0] and bob at [0.2485,0,0].
           If bob is at [0.2485, 0, 0], the rod is [0,0,0] to [0.2485, 0, 0].
           The rod length is 0.2485. 
           The bob center is at [0.2485, 0, 0].
           Let's try that.
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <sensor>
    <jointpos name="pendulum_pos" joint="hinge"/>
  </sensor>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum_base" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="rod" pos="0 0 0">
        <geom name="rod_geom" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
        <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
          <joint name="dummy_joint" type="free"/> <!-- dummy to allow positioning if needed, but we'll use body pos -->
          <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Rod: Length 0.2485, Mass 0, Collision disabled -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <!-- Bob: Sphere, Diameter 0.1 (Radius 0.05), Mass 1.0 kg -->
      <!-- Bob positioned at the end of the rod (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to the hinge -->
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" timestep="0.001"/>

  <worldbody>

      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
